ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON FEB-14-2012 IN DEPT. 503, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PER CCP 36(A) AND 36(D). TRIAL WILL BE SET EARLIER THAN 120 DAYS CONDITIONED ON PLAINTIFFS' AGREEMENT THAT SUMMARY MOTIONS MAY BE HEARD ON 15 DAYS NOTICE WITHOUT AN ADDITIONAL TWO DAYS FOR E-SERVICE. TRIAL IS JUNE 4, 2012 AT 11:15AM IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY MOTIONS IS MAY 24, WITH OPPOSITIONS DUE FIVE COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING AND REPLIES DUE TWO COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING. EXPERT DISCOVERY CUT-OFF DATE IS JUNE 1, 2012. NO LATER THAN MARCH 16, 2012, PLAINTIFFS MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT, THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. NO LATER THAN MARCH 16, 2012, DEFENDANTS MUST PROVIDE A STATEMENT STATING THE NAME AND CONTACT INFORMATION OF EACH PERSON WITH KNOWLEDGE OF ANY FACTS SUPPORTING ANY OF DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. ANY PERSON NOT IDENTIFIED ON THE STATEMENTS WILL BE BARRED FROM TESTIFYING AT TRIAL OR PRESENTING A DECLARATION ON A SUMMARY MOTION ABSENT GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. THE PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING ANY OTHER SCHEDULING OR OTHER ISSUE WHICH ANY OF THEM WANT TO HAVE INCLUDED IN THE PREFERENCE ORDER. FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON MAY 16, 2012 AT 1:30PM. NO NEED FOR ANY PARTY TO FILE ANY CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENT. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY TELEPHONE. APPEARANCES:
Bradley P. Kaplan of ARMSTRONG & ASSOCIATES, LLP (510-433-1830) appearing for defendant / moving party Exxon Mobil Corporation
Anne T. Acuna of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Donald L. Bass, David Bass, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT EXXON MOBIL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Exxon Mobil Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Mr. Manwill's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Bass was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's argument regarding exposure aboard the Exxon Houston is moot. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' claim for premises owner/contractor liability fails even if defendant did not retain control or have knowledge of a preexisting concealed hazardous condition. Defendant's objections to Mr. Manwill's declaration are overruled. The Court treated this as solely a motion for summary judgment as defendant failed to bring a proper motion for summary adjudication. (CRC 3.1350(b), (d).) Order after hearing was signed in open court. An order re: ruling on defendant's amended objections to evidence in opposition was signed in chambers. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kimberly R.
